methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xiongyi Cui and Linxi Fan. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private robotics firms and one public company (ticker 277810.KQ). Top private peers include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable to this analysis. While eleven same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, the dataset contained zero companies matching both seed stage and similar valuation tier simultaneously, and zero at adjacent development stages meeting comparable criteria. A narrower filtered median requires a minimum of three datapoints to be statistically meaningful; applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable selection bias across heterogeneous business models, capital intensities, and market stages within robotics.

Given the limitations of peer-median triangulation, this analysis cannot produce a supported valuation range without additional methods (comparable transactions, DCF analysis, or revenue multiples if applicable data becomes available). The core constraint is insufficient granular comp density at the seed stage within the robotics sector. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed prior fundraising amounts, which would inform pricing trajectory and investor appetite in this specific stage band.
